name: Christmas / Jul
description: Denmark's most celebrated festive season. Danes embrace hygge most intensely during Jul — decorated Christmas trees, æbleskiver (pancake balls), and family gatherings with roast duck or goose

name: Fastelavn
description: Denmark's version of carnival held 7 weeks before Easter. Children dress in costumes and beat a barrel filled with candy (slå katten af tønden). Fastelavn buns (semlor) are traditional

name: Sankt Hans Aften (Midsummer)
description: Celebrated on June 23rd, Danes light enormous bonfires on beaches and open spaces to ward off witches. Communities gather for singing and bonfire rituals across the country

name: Roskilde Festival
description: One of Europe's largest and most prestigious music festivals held annually near Roskilde. Non-profit festival attracting 130,000+ visitors with global headliners across dozens of stages

name: Copenhagen Jazz Festival
description: World-renowned 10-day jazz festival transforming Copenhagen with 1000+ concerts across 100+ venues. Free and ticketed events make jazz accessible to all

name: Aarhus Festival Week
description: Denmark's biggest arts festival held annually in Aarhus featuring theatre, music, visual arts, food and debate. Transforms the entire city into a cultural playground

name: Christmas Markets (Julemarked)
description: Beautiful Christmas markets appear across Danish cities from late November. Copenhagen's Tivoli Christmas Market and Aarhus markets are particularly magical with lights and crafts

name: Valborg / Walpurgis Night
description: Celebrated April 30th, originally a pagan fire festival. University students in particular celebrate with outdoor gatherings and bonfires marking the end of winter
